message:
fix(packs/core/filesystem): block rm -rf \$HOME and \${HOME} variants

`rm -rf \$HOME` and `rm -rf \${HOME}` expand at the shell to the user's
home directory (same effect as `rm -rf ~/`) but slipped past the
rm-rf-root-home Critical rule because the target character class only
accepted \`[/~]\`.

Target class extended to \`(?:[/~]|\\\$\\{?HOME\\b)\` across all three
Critical root/home variants (combined \`-rf\`, separate \`-r -f\`, and
long \`--recursive --force\`). All forms — bare, quoted, braced, and
the quoted-braced \`"\${HOME}"\` — are now blocked.

Regression tests added for every shape.
